refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness (including tele-operation latency), and safety constraints that may impose timing overhead.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under controlled duty cycle conditions with continuous telemetry capture of cycle start/end timestamps, segmented by phase (load, travel, dump). Statistical analysis of p95 percentile against CT_target baseline. Comparison of autonomous vs. tele-operated vs. manual modes if applicable.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump motions with deterministic cycle phase timing.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation subsystem shall maintain operator command responsiveness with latency ≤ latency_budget to avoid cycle time degradation.
FR-2.3: The safety services subsystem shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time penalty.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time telemetry (phase timestamps, total cycle duration) to enable KPI tracking.

candidate_dps: DP-2.A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/electric actuation response time and motion profiles; implement tele-operation with low-latency network link; enforce safety interlocks with minimal timing overhead.
DP-2.B: Implement cycle time prediction and adaptive duty cycle scheduling to meet p95 target through software-based cycle optimization and operator coaching.

DP-2.C: Decompose cycle time into vehicle motion DP, teleoperation responsiveness DP, and safety constraint DP as independent design parameters with explicit timing budgets and integration verification.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2.C: Decompose cycle time into vehicle motion DP, teleoperation responsiveness DP, and safety constraint DP as independent design parameters with explicit timing budgets and integration verification.

potential_sources: Vehicle motion timing is coupled to teleoperation latency: slow operator response or network delay directly increases cycle time.
Safety constraint enforcement (e.g., geofencing, load monitoring) may impose motion delays that conflict with cycle time targets.
Duty cycle definition and site conditions (terrain, weather, operator skill) are external variables that couple to all three sub-DPs.
Telemetry reporting overhead may introduce measurement artifacts or feedback loops that affect actual cycle performance.
coupling_check.notes: DP-2.C acknowledges decomposition but does not eliminate coupling. Recommend explicit coupling matrix and integration test plan at next level. The selected DP is appropriate for hierarchical decomposition but requires rigorous interface definition and timing budget allocation.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle actuation limits and network latency constraints. Risk: If CT_target is unrealistic, no DP can satisfy it; recommend feasibility study.

Assumption: Tele-operator skill and network infrastructure are stable across duty cycles. Risk: Operator variability and network jitter may cause p95 to exceed p50 by significant margin; recommend operator training and network SLA.

Assumption: Safety constraints do not impose hard timing penalties that exceed safety_overhead_budget. Risk: If safety interlocks require motion pauses, cycle time may be uncontrollable; recommend safety-performance trade-off analysis.

Assumption: Telemetry system does not introduce measurement bias or feedback loops. Risk: Instrumentation overhead or operator awareness of timing may alter behavior; recommend blind measurement protocols.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-2.C to explicitly state timing budgets for each sub-DP (vehicle motion budget, teleoperation latency budget, safety overhead budget) and define the integration verification strategy (e.g., end-to-end cycle time simulation, hardware-in-the-loop testing). Add explicit coupling matrix showing dependencies between vehicle, teleoperation, and safety DPs.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution IM is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. It owns the cycle time KPI measurement, reporting, and integration verification. The NGHE Solution IM is responsible for end-to-end cycle time performance and telemetry aggregation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Cycle time metrics (phase timestamps, total duration, p95 statistics) reported to Site Manager and Project Manager for KPI tracking and performance analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Duty cycle definition, CT_target, and operational parameters (site conditions, load profiles) provided by Site Manager and Project Manager to configure cycle time targets and measurement baselines.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety constraints and alerts from Safety Services module that may impose timing overhead; NGHE Solution monitors and reports safety-induced cycle time penalties.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Network connectivity for tele-operation command/response and telemetry transmission; latency and reliability of Network Link directly impact cycle time performance.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level boundary for cycle time KPI ownership. Cycle time is a system-level emergent property that requires integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Minimal diffusion: cycle time measurement and reporting logic is localized to NGHE Solution integration layer.

Vehicle module owns motion actuation timing but does not own teleoperation latency or safety constraint timing. Assigning cycle time KPI to Vehicle alone would diffuse responsibility for network and safety factors. Vehicle is a sub-component, not the system boundary.

Teleoperation module owns operator command responsiveness and network latency but does not own vehicle motion timing or safety constraint timing. Assigning cycle time KPI to Teleoperation alone would diffuse responsibility for vehicle and safety factors. Teleoperation is a sub-component.

Safety Services module owns safety constraint enforcement but does not own vehicle motion or teleoperation responsiveness. Assigning cycle time KPI to Safety Services alone would diffuse responsibility for motion and control factors. Safety Services is a sub-component.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Cycle time KPI is a system-level emergent property that arises from the integrated behavior of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. The NGHE Solution IM boundary is the appropriate owner because it integrates all three sub-modules and is responsible for end-to-end performance verification. Cycle time measurement, telemetry reporting, and integration testing are localized to NGHE Solution, minimizing diffusion.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution IM may become a 'thin integration layer' if cycle time optimization logic is not clearly allocated. Recommend explicit ownership of cycle time measurement, telemetry aggregation, and integration verification within NGHE Solution.

Risk: Coupling between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services may require cross-module timing coordination that is not visible at NGHE Solution level. Recommend explicit coupling matrix and integration test plan.
Risk: Telemetry interface may become a bottleneck if cycle time data is not efficiently aggregated and reported. Recommend telemetry schema design and bandwidth analysis.

Risk: Network Link interface latency is a critical factor in cycle time but is external to NGHE Solution. Recommend explicit SLA for Network Link latency and contingency plans for network degradation.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the NGHE Solution IM scope to explicitly include: (1) cycle time measurement and telemetry aggregation logic, (2) integration verification and end-to-end testing responsibility, (3) timing budget allocation and tracking across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-modules, (4) contingency plans for network latency and safety constraint timing overhead. Define clear ownership boundaries and interface contracts between NGHE Solution and its sub-modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR specifies measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target) and identifies telemetry-based verification, which is good. However, the selected DP-2.C decomposes cycle time into three sub-DPs without explicitly stating how the decomposition maps back to the FR. The DP does not clearly specify the timing budgets, integration strategy, or coupling resolution. Recommend revising DP-2.C to include explicit timing budget allocation and integration verification plan before proceeding to IM assignment.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk: Cycle time measurement logic may be scattered across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules if not explicitly owned by NGHE Solution. Recommend centralizing telemetry aggregation in NGHE Solution IM.

Diffusion Risk: Timing budget tracking and allocation may be diffused across multiple modules if not explicitly managed at NGHE Solution level. Recommend explicit timing budget registry and tracking mechanism.

Diffusion Risk: Integration verification (end-to-end cycle time testing) may be deferred or incomplete if not explicitly assigned to NGHE Solution. Recommend integration test plan as part of NGHE Solution IM scope.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution IM is the appropriate boundary for cycle time KPI ownership. The IM boundary integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-modules and is responsible for end-to-end performance. Cycle time measurement, telemetry reporting, and integration verification are localized to NGHE Solution, minimizing diffusion. However, explicit ownership of these responsibilities must be documented in the IM specification.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is verifiable via instrumented field trials with continuous telemetry capture. Measurement of p95 percentile is feasible with standard statistical methods. Verification requires controlled duty cycle conditions and baseline CT_target definition. Feasibility is high if telemetry infrastructure is in place.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time KPI is allocated to NGHE Solution IM with clear ownership of measurement, reporting, and integration verification. Sub-FRs are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-modules with explicit timing budgets. Allocation clarity is good, but requires explicit timing budget documentation and integration test plan.

recommended_revisions: Revise DP-2.C to explicitly state timing budgets for each sub-DP (vehicle motion budget, teleoperation latency budget, safety overhead budget) and define the integration verification strategy.
Add explicit coupling matrix showing dependencies between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services DPs.
Define telemetry schema for cycle time measurement (phase timestamps, total duration, p95 statistics) and specify telemetry reporting frequency and latency requirements.
Specify acceptance criteria for each sub-FR (FR-2.1, FR-2.2, FR-2.3, FR-2.4) with measurable metrics and verification methods.
Document assumptions and risks related to CT_target feasibility, operator skill variability, network latency, and safety constraint timing overhead.

decision.justification: The refined FR is well-defined with measurable acceptance criteria and clear verification approach. The selected DP-2.C appropriately decomposes cycle time into three sub-DPs (vehicle motion, teleoperation responsiveness, safety constraint) that map to the sub-FRs. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is the correct boundary for cycle time KPI ownership with appropriate external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). However, the DP-2.C lacks explicit timing budget allocation and integration verification strategy, which are critical for managing coupling and ensuring feasibility. Recommend proceeding to the next decomposition level (FR-2.1, FR-2.2, FR-2.3, FR-2.4) with explicit timing budget constraints and integration test plan. The gate review identified no blocking issues, but recommended revisions should be incorporated before detailed design.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump motions with deterministic cycle phase timing ≤ vehicle_motion_budget.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation subsystem shall maintain operator command responsiveness with latency ≤ teleoperation_latency_budget to avoid cycle time degradation.
FR-2.3: The safety services subsystem shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time penalty.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time telemetry (phase timestamps, total cycle duration, p95 statistics) via Telemetry interface.

revise.why: DP-2.C requires explicit timing budget allocation (vehicle_motion_budget, teleoperation_latency_budget, safety_overhead_budget) and integration verification strategy (end-to-end cycle time simulation, hardware-in-the-loop testing, field trial protocol) before proceeding to detailed design. The DP should also include explicit coupling matrix and resolution strategy for dependencies between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-DPs.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle actuation limits and network latency constraints.
Tele-operator skill and network infrastructure are stable across duty cycles.
Safety constraints do not impose hard timing penalties that exceed safety_overhead_budget.
Telemetry system does not introduce measurement bias or feedback loops.
Duty cycle definition and site conditions are stable and well-characterized.
p95 percentile is the appropriate KPI metric for cycle time performance (vs. mean, p50, or p99).
open_risks: Risk: CT_target may be unrealistic given vehicle actuation limits and network latency. Mitigation: Conduct feasibility study with vehicle dynamics simulation and network latency analysis.
Risk: Operator variability may cause p95 to exceed p50 by significant margin. Mitigation: Implement operator training program and skill assessment.
Risk: Safety constraints may impose hard timing penalties that exceed safety_overhead_budget. Mitigation: Conduct safety-performance trade-off analysis and define acceptable safety overhead.
Risk: Network latency may be unpredictable or exceed latency_budget under site conditions. Mitigation: Implement network SLA and contingency plans for network degradation.
Risk: Telemetry measurement overhead may introduce bias or feedback loops. Mitigation: Implement blind measurement protocols and validate telemetry accuracy.
evidence_needed: Vehicle dynamics simulation results showing achievable cycle phase timing under duty cycle conditions.
Network latency analysis showing achievable teleoperation responsiveness under site conditions.
Safety constraint timing analysis showing acceptable safety overhead budget.
Telemetry schema design and bandwidth analysis for cycle time measurement and reporting.
Operator skill assessment and training program design.
Field trial protocol for p95 cycle time measurement and KPI validation.
Coupling matrix and integration verification strategy for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-modules.
